description: Create a content slide on quantum computing with a right sidebar callout and a two-column layout comparing qubits versus bits using explanatory text blocks.

header.headline: As the cornerstone for many future technologies, quantum computing will revolutionize hardware by radically increasing computing power
header.sub: Qubits beat bits – A single qubit is enough to represent numbers of almost any size

main.title: Technical difference between qubits and bits
main.body: Superposition state vs. binary state. Qubit: Quantum computer. 1 qubit. Probability of being in state 1 set to 24.3%, which in decimal notation is 0.243; multiplied by 1,000 gives 243. Another example shows 1 qubit with probability of being in state 1 set to 10.37982%, which in decimal notation begins 0.10. The slide contrasts a qubit's superposition with a bit's binary state.

items.text: Quantum computing uses qubits rather than bits, allowing information to exist in superposition instead of only binary states.
A single qubit can represent values with very fine granularity through probability amplitudes.
This capability underpins the expectation that quantum computing will radically increase computing power for future technologies.
